raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased a XXXX XXXX '' XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX monitor on XX/XX/year>. On XX/XX/XXXX, the monitor became defective a portion of the screen went black and unresponsive. I reported the issue to XXXX and they arranged an in-home repair for XX/XX/XXXX. After the repair, the screen remained defective. I reported this the same day and requested a return. I was told the ticket would remain open, but it was closed without resolution. 

Since then, Ive contacted XXXX multiple times to return the product for a refund. I was repeatedly denied a return or refund and told that XXXX warranty policy only allows for repairs. I made it clear I do not want a second repair attempt, as the first one failed. I am not asking for a replacement I want to return the defective product and be refunded. 

I submitted a dispute through American Express. I provided documentation, photos, and a formal letter. XXXX responded by citing their 15-day return policy and the fact that I still have the monitor. However, they have refused to authorize a return or provide an XXXX, leaving me with no path to send it back. I have offered multiple times to return the item. I am not trying to keep the product and get a refund I simply want a way to return a defective product that XXXX has failed to fix. 

I also contacted XXXX corporate. They replied in writing that only a repair would be allowed, and that a refund is not offered under their policy even if the repair fails. This contradicts federal warranty protections and has left me stuck with a broken {$1500.00} product. I am requesting that American Express be required to reverse the charge or that XXXX be required to accept the return and issue a refund.
